content: makefile-hierarchical-help: Makefile with categorized help output (Getting Started, Development, Testing, Quality Gates, Database, AWS LocalStack, Utilities) improves discoverability. Default target shows full help menu with 25+ commands organized by purpose.

env-example-bun-hot-reload: Bun's --watch flag (packages/chat-gateway: bun run --watch src/server.ts) provides zero-config TypeScript hot reload for local development. No nodemon/tsx/ts-node-dev needed.

name: seed-data-json-fixtures
description: JSON fixture files (tenants.json, skills.json) + TypeScript seeder script with @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb provides type-safe test data initialization. Seeder supports --create-tables, --seed-data, --delete-tables flags for flexible workflows.

content: monorepo-cli-integration: CLI packages must be added to root package.json scripts: build:cli target, test:cli target, and lint:packages command chain. Workspace dependency references use 'workspace:*' for @chimera/shared and @chimera/core.

name: cli-package-structure
description: CLI packages in Chimera monorepo use Commander.js for command parsing, chalk for colored output, ora for spinners, inquirer for prompts, and table for tabular output. Package structure: bin/chimera (executable wrapper) → dist/cli.js (compiled from src/cli.ts). Command modules in src/commands/ register subcommands via register*Commands(program) pattern. Config stored in ~/.chimera/config.json via utils/config.ts.

content: aws-sdk-peerdependencies: AWS SDK v3 clients defined as peerDependencies in packages/core/package.json. Discovery module adds @aws-sdk/client-cost-explorer, @aws-sdk/client-resource-groups-tagging-api, @aws-sdk/client-config. Pattern: Dynamic imports (await import('@aws-sdk/...')) within methods to avoid requiring deps at module load time.

name: activity-logging-decision-capture
description: Activity logging module captures agent decision-making with DecisionLog type (question, alternatives, selected option, justification, confidence), WellArchitectedEvaluation mapping decisions to 6 AWS pillars (Operational Excellence, Security, Reliability, Performance Efficiency, Cost Optimization, Sustainability) with 0-10 scores, DecisionLogger service class with calculateConfidence() (score gap + diversity), determineRiskLevel() (low/medium/high/critical), queryDecisions() filtering, and generateAnalytics() aggregating patterns. Pattern: preserve agent mental model (all alternatives not just winner), multi-criteria decision analysis with pillar weights, cost transparency (immediate/monthly/per-transaction), DynamoDB storage with PK=TENANT#{id} SK=ACTIVITY#{timestamp}#{activityId}, TTL for retention. Enables post-hoc why-did-agent-choose-X queries and continuous learning from decision patterns.

content: Standard DynamoDB service class structure: (1) DynamoDBClient interface for dependency injection, (2) Config interface with tableName and dynamodb client, (3) Service class with CRUD methods, (4) Helper methods for PK/SK formatting (e.g., TENANT#{id}, ACTION#{timestamp}#{id}), (5) TTL calculation methods (Unix timestamp), (6) Error handling for ConditionalCheckFailedException on put operations, (7) Query methods with KeyConditionExpression and optional FilterExpression, (8) GSI queries with IndexName parameter

name: activity-dashboard-aggregation
description: StatusDashboard class aggregates real-time agent activity from sessions table: (1) session metrics (active/idle/terminated counts, total messages, token usage), (2) agent-level summaries with per-agent metrics, (3) pagination with base64-encoded tokens, (4) tenant-wide dashboard combining all metrics. Follows CostTracker pattern: DynamoDB client interface, config object, public query methods, private aggregation helpers.

content: AWS SDK peerDependencies for infra-builder: @aws-sdk/client-codecommit, @aws-sdk/client-codepipeline, @aws-sdk/client-cloudformation added to packages/core/package.json peerDependencies following established pattern to avoid version conflicts when consuming @chimera/core.
AWS SDK v3 clients added as peerDependencies in packages/core/package.json. Package name correction: @aws-sdk/client-config (incorrect) -> @aws-sdk/client-config-service (correct)

name: mutable-builder-readonly-return
description: When building objects with readonly properties incrementally, use mutable temporary type during construction, then return as readonly type. Example: type MutableAccountCost without readonly, build with mutations, return as AccountCostBreakdown with readonly. TypeScript structural typing makes mutable type assignable to readonly type.

content: well-architected-sdk-peerdependency: @aws-sdk/client-wellarchitected added to packages/core/package.json peerDependencies following established pattern for AWS SDK v3 clients. All AWS SDK clients are peer dependencies to avoid version conflicts across monorepo.

aws-sdk-dependencies-pattern: AWS SDK v3 clients must be peerDependencies only, never regular dependencies. This prevents version conflicts and allows consuming packages to control SDK versions. Move all @aws-sdk/client-* packages from dependencies to peerDependencies section.

name: barrel-export-conflict-resolution
description: Wildcard exports (export *) in barrel files cause namespace collisions when multiple modules define types with the same name. Solution: Replace with explicit exports and rename conflicting types using 'as' keyword. Common conflicts: RiskLevel (activity vs well-architected have different semantics), ISOTimestamp (multiple modules define string), StageStatus (swarm refinement vs pipeline execution), AgentRole (orchestration coordination vs swarm problem-solving).

name: dynamodb-query-params-typing
description: Replace 'params: any' with explicit TypeScript types for DynamoDB query/scan parameters. Define interface with TableName, IndexName?, KeyConditionExpression?, FilterExpression?, ExpressionAttributeValues, ExpressionAttributeNames?, Limit? fields to catch type errors at compile time.

name: aws-sdk-v3-type-mapping
description: AWS SDK v3 enum/type conversions: CostMetric (UnblendedCost → UNBLENDED_COST), FileModeTypeEnum (NORMAL/EXECUTABLE/SYMLINK only), GroupDefinition (Type/Key capitalized). SDK response properties often missing from types, require 'as any' cast.

name: eslint-flat-config-compatibility
description: ESLint 10 uses flat config (eslint.config.js), ESLint 8 uses .eslintrc.json. When packages have mixed versions, use ESLint 8 with .eslintrc.json for compatibility. Remove --ext flag from lint scripts for flat config support.

name: typescript-incremental-build-staleness
description: TypeScript tsbuildinfo can become stale in git worktrees, causing false 'file not built from source' errors. Solution: rm tsconfig.tsbuildinfo before rebuilding packages/shared.

name: bun-test-path-scoping
description: Use './directory/' prefix for path-based test scoping. 'bun test packages/ infra/' treats arguments as filename filters and still searches all workspaces. Must use 'bun test ./packages/ ./infra/' to properly scope tests to specific directories.

content: Root package.json test script must scope to 'bun test ./packages/ ./infra/' to exclude integration/e2e tests that require a running server. Integration tests available via separate scripts (test:integration, test:e2e).

AWS SDK tool files use retryWithBackoff<T> generic method. TypeScript cannot infer T through overloaded SDK send() methods. All retryWithBackoff calls must specify explicit CommandOutput type parameters (e.g., retryWithBackoff<PutObjectCommandOutput>).

name: strands-sdk-type-shim
description: When importing from unpublished npm packages (@strands-agents/sdk), create ambient module declaration (.d.ts) files to provide TypeScript types. Pattern: (1) Create packages/core/src/<package>-shim.d.ts with 'declare module' block, (2) Define exported interfaces and functions matching expected API, (3) Force-add to git with 'git add -f' to override *.d.ts gitignore rule. Allows TypeScript compilation without actual package installed.

name: strands-tool-factory-pattern
description: AWS tool conversion from class-based OOP to functional Strands tools. Pattern: (1) Factory functions capture clientFactory in closure, (2) Each tool uses tool() decorator with Zod input schema + async callback, (3) Flatten context params (tenantId/agentId/region) directly into Zod schema instead of separate AWSToolContext object, (4) Return JSON.stringify for all results (success + error), (5) Binary data handled via base64 encoding/decoding (S3 body, Lambda zipFile). Tools become discrete LLM choices rather than methods on class instance. 24 tools across 4 AWS services.

content: strands-ts-sdk-not-published: @strands-agents/sdk (npm) is at v0.0.1-development and NOT published to npm yet (as of 2026-03-21). Use declare module shim for TypeScript type-checking. Package uses zod v4 (^4.1.12) but zod v3 is compatible for schema definitions. Import: import { tool } from '@strands-agents/sdk'. Tool format: tool({ name, description, inputSchema: z.object({...}), callback: async (input) => string }).

name: in-memory-test-client
description: Test implementations use Map-based storage for fast, dependency-free testing. InMemoryClient implements MemoryClient interface with Map<string, MemoryEntry> for entries and Map<string, SessionState> for sessions. Strategy selection uses simple heuristics (content.includes checks). Pattern: constructor(namespace), async methods, clear separation from production clients via factory.

name: namespace-parsing-non-greedy-regex
description: Namespace parsing with IDs containing hyphens requires non-greedy regex. Pattern /^tenant-(.+?)-user-(.+)$/ uses .+? (non-greedy) instead of .+ to correctly extract 'tenant-123' from 'tenant-tenant-123-user-user-456'. Greedy .+ would capture 'tenant-123-user' as tenantId. Critical for multi-tenant isolation - parsing errors could leak data.

name: memory-client-tier-config-pattern
description: TypeScript memory integration mirrors Python agent pattern — InMemoryClient for testing, tier-based config (Basic STM=10, Advanced STM=50, Premium STM=200), strategy selection (SUMMARY all, USER_PREFERENCE advanced+, SEMANTIC premium), auto message storage in ChimeraAgent.invoke(). MemoryClientFactory uses lazy require() to avoid circular imports.

content: Memory namespace parseNamespace() regex must use non-greedy (.+?) for tenantId to handle hyphenated IDs correctly. Greedy (.+) would consume hyphens meant for the -user- separator boundary. E.g. tenant-my-tenant-user-bob: greedy captures my-tenant-user-bob as tenantId, non-greedy captures my-tenant.

name: react-loop-bedrock-integration
description: ReAct loop implementation for LLM agents with Bedrock Converse API. Pattern: (1) Build messages array with user message, (2) Loop max 10 iterations calling model.converse(), (3) Check stopReason - if 'end_turn' extract text and break, if 'tool_use' find toolUse blocks in response.output.message.content, (4) Execute matched tools via callback(input), (5) Add assistant message + tool_result messages to conversation, (6) Continue loop. Tool specs converted from Zod schema to JSON Schema for Bedrock API. Backward compatible - returns placeholder when no model provided.

name: mock-model-test-pattern
description: MockModel test pattern for deterministic agent testing. MockModel accepts array of MockResponse (text, toolCalls, stopReason) and simulates Bedrock Converse API. Test structure: (1) Create mock tools with Zod schemas and callbacks, (2) Configure MockModel with response sequence (toolCallResponse → textResponse for 2-turn ReAct), (3) Pass model + loadedTools to agent config, (4) Assert on result.output, result.toolCalls, and model.getCallCount(). Acceptance test validates full pipeline: user request → tool execution → final response. InMemoryClient.retrieve({limit: N}) used to verify memory storage instead of getRecentMessages().

content: react-loop-pattern-ts: ChimeraAgent.invoke() implements ReAct loop when model is injected via AgentConfig.model. Loop: build messages → model.converse() → check tool_use → execute tool.callback() → feed toolResult back → repeat until end_turn or max_iterations(10). Backward compatible: returns placeholder when no model. MockModel from tests/helpers/mock-model.ts satisfies the model interface for deterministic testing.

name: bedrock-model-adapter-pattern
description: BedrockModel class implements same interface as MockModel (converse() returning ConverseResponse with output.message.content[]) for agent integration. Converts between ChimeraAgent ContentBlock format and AWS Bedrock native format. Added as peerDependency @aws-sdk/client-bedrock-runtime^3.0.0.

content: chat-gateway-config-pattern: Chat gateway uses singleton config loaded from environment (BEDROCK_MODEL_ID, BEDROCK_REGION, BEDROCK_ENABLED, etc). BedrockModel created conditionally based on config.bedrock.enabled flag, falling back to placeholder responses when disabled.

name: bun-monorepo-docker-build
description: Multi-stage Dockerfile for Bun monorepos: (1) builder stage installs all workspace deps and builds packages in dependency order, (2) runtime stage copies only production deps and dist artifacts, (3) runs as non-root user (chimera:1001), (4) includes wget-based health check on /health endpoint.

name: bedrock-model-adapter-pattern
description: BedrockModel class wraps AWS Bedrock Converse API with bidirectional content block conversion. Config-driven feature toggle via BEDROCK_ENABLED env var.

name: skill-md-parser-bridge
description: SKILL.md v2 parser uses js-yaml for robust frontmatter parsing (YAML + markdown body). Skill bridge loads skills from registry, converts to agent tools (instruction → prompt injection, MCP → callable tools). SystemPromptTemplate requires class instantiation (new SystemPromptTemplate(template)). SkillRegistry.getInstalledSkills() returns SkillInstall[], not string[]. Tests cover parsing (permissions, dependencies, MCP config, tests) and agent integration (tool conversion, prompt enhancement, catalog building).

name: glob-to-regex-conversion
description: Safe glob-to-regex conversion pattern: (1) Replace glob wildcards with temporary placeholders (** → <!DOUBLESTAR!>, * → <!STAR!>, ? → <!QUESTION!>), (2) Escape special regex characters [.+^()|[\]\\], (3) Replace placeholders with regex equivalents (** → .*, * → [^/]*, ? → [^/]). This prevents regex syntax errors from wildcards being escaped before conversion. Used for filesystem permission pattern matching in SkillTrustEngine.matchGlob().

content: DynamoDB client interface must include update() method for UpdateExpression queries. Using put() for UpdateExpression operations (like incrementing counters) is incorrect - put() replaces entire item, update() modifies specific attributes. SkillRegistry uses update() for recordUsage() (increment use_count) and incrementDownloadCount() (increment download_count).

name: skill-ecosystem-three-builder-decomposition
description: Phase 3 skill activation decomposed into 3 parallel builders with non-overlapping file scopes: (1) parser+bridge (parser.ts, skill-bridge.ts, agent.ts), (2) registry+security (registry.ts, validator.ts, trust-engine.ts), (3) MCP+tests (mcp-gateway-client.ts, installer.ts, test files). Key: send specs via mail when Write blocked by hooks, instruction skills inject into prompt while MCP skills create callable tools.

name: load-test-scaling-strategy
description: Scaling load tests from 50 to 1000 concurrent sessions requires: (1) Multiple concurrency distributions (100×10, 200×5, 500×2, 1000×1) to test different patterns, (2) Gradual ramp-up test to identify bottlenecks, (3) Cost validation via sample extrapolation (run 100 clients, estimate 1000-client cost), (4) Skill usage tests at scale, (5) Auto-scaling verification. Each test has SLA expectations: 90% success rate, p99 latency thresholds, error rate <5-20% depending on concurrency.

name: lead-agent-two-builder-parallel
description: For production-hardening tasks with CDK infra + docs/tests scope, split into (1) CDK builder for infra changes (observability-stack.ts), (2) Docs builder for runbooks + DR docs + load tests. Non-overlapping file scope prevents merge conflicts. Both complete in ~10 min.

name: evolution-module-testing-pattern
description: Unit tests for evolution modules use Bun mock() to stub AWS SDK clients (DynamoDBDocumentClient, S3Client, CodeCommitClient, VerifiedPermissionsClient). Pattern: (1) Mock client.send() with @ts-expect-error comment, (2) Return Promise.resolve() with expected shape, (3) Verify behavior via expect() on result values. SafetyHarness methods also mocked for authorization flow testing.

content: AWS SDK evolution dependencies: For evolution modules (auto-skill-gen, iac-modifier, safety-harness), root package.json devDependencies must include: @aws-sdk/client-codecommit, @aws-sdk/client-dynamodb, @aws-sdk/client-s3, @aws-sdk/client-verifiedpermissions, @aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb. These are peerDependencies in packages/core but required as devDependencies for test execution.

agent-self-edit-tools-pattern: Python CodeCommit/CodePipeline @tool wrappers follow same pattern as s3_tools.py/ec2_tools.py - boto3 client inside function, @tool decorator, try/except returning strings. Tools split by service: codecommit_tools.py (5 repo ops), codepipeline_tools.py (7 pipeline ops). All registered in chimera_agent.py load_tenant_tools() function.

name: aws-sdk-v3-type-safety
description: AWS SDK v3 requires explicit enum imports (LanguageCode.EN_US not string 'en-US'). Response properties are optional and need null checks. Array operations (filter/map) on AWS types (Block[]) require explicit type annotations to satisfy TypeScript strict mode. Always import type/enum from SDK rather than using string literals.

name: lead-decomposition-for-new-module
description: When adding a new AWS service integration, decompose into (1) builder-tools: tool files + client-factory + tool-utils + index + peerDeps, following the existing Strands tool pattern from s3-tool.ts, and (2) builder-processor: higher-level module with types, processor class, tests, core index exports, architecture doc. Non-overlapping file scope ensures clean merge. Send full spec via ov mail body since lead hooks prevent file writes.

content: dynamodb-client-proper-types: DynamoDB client interfaces must use proper AWS SDK types instead of 'any'. Import GetCommandInput, GetCommandOutput, PutCommandInput, PutCommandOutput, QueryCommandInput, QueryCommandOutput, etc. from '@aws-sdk/lib-dynamodb'. Maintains type safety for dependency-injected clients.

name: security-fix-decomposition-by-package
description: When fixing cross-cutting security issues across a monorepo, decompose builders by package boundary (core/gateway/infra) rather than by issue type. Each builder handles all CRITICALs for their package. This gives non-overlapping file scope naturally while ensuring complete coverage.

name: aws-tools-infrastructure-preparation
description: Shared infrastructure files (client-factory.ts, tool-utils.ts, index.ts, package.json) prepared before individual tool files. Pattern: (1) Add client imports and AWSClient union type, (2) Add getXClient() method using getOrCreateClient generic, (3) Add SERVICE_RETRYABLE_ERRORS constant, (4) Add commented export for tool factory, (5) Add peerDependency. Enables parallel development of 21 tool files by other builders.

content: tier3-aws-tool-pattern: Tier 3 data/analytics tools (RDS, Redshift, Glue, Athena, OpenSearch) follow same factory pattern as Tier 1 tools. Each tool file exports createXxxTools(clientFactory) returning 6-operation tool array. Standard operations: describe/list (query metadata), create (provision resource), delete (cleanup), start/stop or pause/resume (lifecycle), modify/update (configuration changes). All use Zod input schemas with standard tenantId/agentId/region fields.

aws-tool-error-constants-centralization: All AWS service retryable error constants defined in tool-utils.ts (RDS_RETRYABLE_ERRORS, REDSHIFT_RETRYABLE_ERRORS, etc.), not duplicated in individual tool files. Tool files import constants via 'import { retryWithBackoff, formatToolError, XXX_RETRYABLE_ERRORS } from ./tool-utils'. formatToolError() aggregates all error types for consistent retry detection across services.

name: data-service-pause-resume-pattern
description: RDS and Redshift tools include pause/resume operations for cost optimization (compute charges stop when paused, storage charges continue). Critical for agents managing tenant resources autonomously — allows cost-aware scheduling (pause during off-hours, resume on-demand). Pattern: pauseXxx/resumeXxx tools with single clusterIdentifier/dbInstanceIdentifier input, return current state in response.

content: aws-tool-tag-format-variance: CodeBuild Tag type uses lowercase 'key'/'value' fields, not 'Key'/'Value'. Must transform createResourceTags() output: tags.map(t => ({ key: t.Key, value: t.Value }))

sagemaker-runtime-split: SageMaker InvokeEndpoint requires separate @aws-sdk/client-sagemaker-runtime package, not included in @aws-sdk/client-sagemaker. Model management and endpoint operations in base client, inference in runtime client.

blocked-tool-placeholder-pattern: When infrastructure missing (client factory getters, retryable error constants), create placeholder tool file with: (1) clear error message documenting requirements, (2) copy-paste implementation instructions, (3) single placeholder tool returning error to maintain factory signature.

name: aws-sdk-tool-file-pattern
description: Each AWS tool file follows: (1) factory function createXxxTools(clientFactory) returning Tool[], (2) imports from ./strands-agents (tool), zod (z), ./client-factory (AWSClientFactory, createResourceTags), ./tool-utils (retryWithBackoff, formatToolError, XXX_RETRYABLE_ERRORS), (3) each tool has tenantId/agentId/region in inputSchema, (4) callback: startTime, get client via factory, build Command, retryWithBackoff, return JSON.stringify({success, data, metadata}), catch formatToolError. Average 300-500 lines per service, 4-8 operations per service.

content: test-peer-dependency-modules: When testing modules with AWS SDK peer dependencies (@aws-sdk/*), avoid importing implementation files that load SDK at top level. Instead: (1) Test only type definitions and enums from separate files, or (2) Create mock implementations inline. Example: event-bus.ts imports @aws-sdk/client-eventbridge, so tests import only from event-types.ts.

mock-dynamodb-update-accumulation: DynamoDB update mock must clone items and nested objects to avoid reference issues. Use (item.field || 0) + value to simulate if_not_exists(field, 0) + value behavior for proper accumulation across multiple calls.

cognito-token-validity-units: CDK converts Duration.hours()/days() to minutes internally in CloudFormation template. Test assertions must use minute values: 1 hour = 60 min, 8 hours = 480 min, 30 days = 43200 min.
cognito-auth-flows-allow-prefix: CDK adds ALLOW_ prefix to Cognito ExplicitAuthFlows. Test for ALLOW_USER_SRP_AUTH not USER_SRP_AUTH.

name: cli-deployment-orchestration
description: CLI commands orchestrate AWS deployment: (1) deploy - creates CodeCommit repo, pushes source, runs bootstrap script, saves config, (2) connect - queries CloudFormation outputs, saves API/WebSocket URLs + Cognito IDs to ~/.chimera/config.json, (3) status - displays stack health, pipeline status with table formatting

content: cli-aws-sdk-peerdependencies: CLI package declares @aws-sdk/client-codecommit, @aws-sdk/client-codepipeline, @aws-sdk/client-cloudformation as peerDependencies (not dependencies) following aws-sdk-dependencies-pattern from core packages

cli-deploy-flow-pattern: CLI deploy flow uses execSync for git/CDK operations and AWS SDK v3 for API calls (CodeCommit, CloudFormation). STS auth via aws CLI execSync, CodeCommit repo creation via SDK, CDK deploy via bootstrap.sh with input sanitization (regex whitelist). Config persists DeploymentConfig with account/region/endpoints/status to ~/.chimera/config.json.

lead-agent-hook-constraints: Lead agents in overstory are restricted to read-only file operations plus ov/sd/mulch/git-read commands. Cannot use Edit, Write, Agent, TaskCreate, or file-modifying bash commands. Must delegate all implementation to builder agents via ov sling. Specs must be sent via ov mail (not spec files) due to Write block.

description: barrel-file-ai-prose-corruption: AI model wrote conversational prose into packages/core/src/aws-tools/index.ts instead of TypeScript code, breaking all aws-tools imports across @chimera/core
resolution: Rewrote index.ts with proper re-exports for all 19 tool factories, client-factory, types, and tool-utils. No package.json changes needed (deps already present).

content: lead-agent-hook-constraints: Lead agents in overstory are restricted to read-only file operations plus ov/sd/ml/git-read commands. Cannot git merge, commit, add, rebase, push, or write files. Must delegate all file modifications to builder agents and signal merge_ready for coordinator to handle git operations.
